title: Edmonton's Most Iconic Neighbourhoods: A Local's Guide to Whyte Ave, Oliver, Glenora, and the Highlands
excerpt: Edmonton is a city that wears its character on its streets. From the buzzing patios of Whyte Avenue to the quiet, tree-lined lanes of Glenora, each neighbourhood tells a different story about who Edmontonians are and what they love about their city.
author: Culture Alberta

title: Alberta to Vote on Immigration This Fall, But Is Danielle Smith Backtracking Previous Views?
excerpt: Premier Danielle Smith made a bold move Thursday night, announcing Albertans will head to the polls on October 19 for a referendum on immigration and constitutional reform. It's the kind of announcement that dominates headlines and that's partly the point.
author: Culture Alberta

title: Red Deer RCMP Search for Missing Teen as Temperatures Drop to -20°C
excerpt: A 16-year-old girl has been missing from Red Deer for two days and with temperatures plunging to -20°C, every hour counts.
author: Culture Alberta

title: Edmonton Once Had a Waterfall Pouring Off the High Level Bridge, And We Still Miss It
excerpt: For nearly 30 years, a man-made waterfall poured off the High Level Bridge 64 metres straight down into the North Saskatchewan River, seven metres taller than Niagara Falls. Kayakers paddled through it. Rainbows formed beneath it. And then one day, the city quietly turned it off forever. Here's the story Edmonton forgot
author: Culture Alberta

title: Alberta Drops $400 Million on New Water Bombers, And They're Being Built Right Here in Alberta
excerpt: Alberta's spending $400 million on five new water bombers but you won't see a single one until 2031
author: Culture Alberta

title: Edmonton Activates Extreme Weather Response as City Faces Two Days of Heavy Snow and a Plunge to -20s
excerpt: Edmonton is bracing for one of the more brutal cold snaps of the season and the city isn't waiting around.

title: Alberta Drivers, Brace Yourselves: Traffic Fines Are Going Up Next Month
excerpt: Attention all Alberta drivers, March is bringing more than just the end of winter it's bringing heavier hits to your wallet if you get pulled over.

title: Homeless Edmonton Man Goes Viral After Challenging Alberta Separatist's Claims
excerpt: A powerful street encounter in Edmonton has captured thousands of views online, sparking conversations about political rhetoric versus real-world action

title: Missing Teen in Camrose: Police Ask Public to Help Find 16-Year-Old Bobby
excerpt: Camrose Police are looking for a teenage boy who took off from his mom's car near the police station this afternoon. He doesn't have money or a phone, and officers want to make sure he's safe.

title: Alberta Gas Prices Dip Below the Dollar Mark - Fill Up While You Can
excerpt: Gas prices in Alberta have dropped below $1 per litre at stations across Edmonton, Calgary, and rural areas. Here's where to find the cheapest fuel and why prices are so low heading into 2026

title: A 44-year-old father of three died after waiting more than 8 hours at Grey Nuns Hospital in Edmonton
excerpt: Prashant Sreekumar waited 8 hours at Edmonton's Grey Nuns Hospital with severe chest pain. He collapsed and died before seeing a doctor. His family is demanding answers as Alberta's ER wait times reach dangerous levels
author: Culture Alberta

title: Attention Edmonton Northsiders: A New Costco Is Coming to Manning Town Centre
excerpt: A new Costco warehouse is coming to Manning Town Centre at 153 Avenue and Manning Drive, giving Northside Edmonton shoppers a long-awaited alternative to the packed Yellowhead location. If timelines hold, expect doors to open around August 2026
author: Culture Alberta

title: Coronation Park Sports and Recreation Centre Set to Open Ahead of Schedule in Edmonton
excerpt: Edmonton's Coronation Park Sports and Recreation Centre opens January 27, 2026 North America's first indoor triathlon training facility featuring a 50-metre pool, 250-metre velodrome, and running track all under one roof.

title: Police Hunting for Man Who Sexually Assaulted Women at West Edmonton Mall

title: Alberta Businesses Claim They Can't Find Managers - Locals Aren't Buying It
excerpt: Employers say they can't find Canadians for $40/hr management jobs. Locals say that's news to them. The TFW program is under the microscope.

title: Racist Graffiti Plagues Brooks as RCMP Hunt for Vandals
excerpt: A spray pad where kids cool off in summer. A flower pot on main street. Bathroom walls at local parks. These are the places where someone has been scrawling white supremacist messages across Brooks, and police want to know who's responsible.
author: Culture Alberta

title: Edmonton Braces for Brutal Cold Snap as City Opens Warming Centres
excerpt: Winter isn't playing nice this year. Edmonton triggered its extreme weather response Monday as a brutal cold snap threatens to send temperatures plummeting below -30  by week's end.
author: Culture Alberta

title: Edmonton's Tax Problem: Property Tax Hike While Calgary Keeps Rates Low
excerpt: Edmonton city council just approved a 6.9% property tax hike for 2026 capping four years of increases that add up to roughly 30%. Meanwhile, Calgary held theirs to 1.6%. Same province, very different outcomes.
author: Culture Alberta

title: Dave Chappelle's Edmonton show goes off the rails

title: Quick-Thinking Staff Save Student's Life at South Edmonton School
excerpt: Staff at a South Edmonton Catholic school are being credited with saving a student’s life after a medical emergency on the playground.During recess last week at St. Thomas Aquinas Catholic School, a student went into sudden cardiac arrest.

title: Edmonton Votes to Demolish Argyll Velodrome
excerpt: Edmonton Council approved $3.2 million to demolish the Argyll Velodrome despite five councilors voting to save it.

title: Edmonton Woman Loses Both Legs, Now Inspires Thousands from Hospital
excerpt: On a routine October workday, Raelyn Janssen was doing what she always did working hard at a landscaping job in St. Albert. Minutes later, she was fighting for her life.An SUV struck a parked trailer, pinning the 23-year-old beneath it.
author: Culture Alberta

title: YEGWAVE Confirmed Not to Be Based in Alberta, Despite Strong Local Presence
excerpt: YEGWAVE, a large Edmonton based Culture Page, appears to list its location as the Netherlands on public account data, prompting discussion about local authenticity and representation.
author: Culture Alberta

title: Community Mourns After Fatal Assault in Central Edmonton
excerpt: A 55-year-old Edmonton man, identified as Arvi Singh Sagoo, has died after an assault near 109 Street and 100 Avenue. Police have charged Kyle Papin, 40, and say further charges may follow. Sagoo is remembered as a kind father whose death has left the community grieving and rallying in support of his two children.
author: Culture Alberta

title: Alberta Invokes the Notwithstanding Clause to End Historic Teachers’ Strike
excerpt: Alberta Premier Danielle Smith has invoked the notwithstanding clause to end the province’s largest-ever teachers’ strike forcing 51,000 educators back to work. The move has sparked backlash from unions and critics, especially after an old 2006 op-ed resurfaced where Smith wrote that the clause was “simply not necessary.”
author: Culture Alberta

title: Caught on Camera: Edmonton Delivery Driver Pretends to Drop Off Order, Then Takes It Back
excerpt: A viral security video from Edmonton has sparked frustration among local residents after showing a food delivery driver appearing to fake a drop-off snapping the confirmation photo, then walking away with the meal still in hand.
author: Culture Alberta

title: Edmonton Designer Maria Wozniak Showcases Alberta Creativity on National Stage
excerpt: Edmonton’s Maria Augusta Wozniak is one of 16 designers in Canada chosen for the 2025 Cashmere Collection, creating a couture dress from bathroom tissue to raise funds for breast cancer research. Vote...

title: Must-Eat Places in Edmonton: Where to Grab a Bite You’ll Never Forget
excerpt: Edmonton isn’t just about hockey, the river valley, or long winters it’s also a seriously underrated food city. From late-night shawarma to brunch spots that will make you forget about your diet, here are some must-eat places that every foodie (and hungry visitor) needs to try.
author: Culture Alberta

title: Muted Mic, Louder Message: Alberta Student Silenced for Asking About Teachers’ Strike
excerpt: During an Alberta Next panel, a student’s microphone was muted as he tried to ask about the looming teachers’ strike. The moment has sparked debate about student voices, accountability, and whether young Albertans are being shut out of conversations that affect them most.
author: Culture Alberta

title: How Alberta Slid from Canada’s Highest to Its Lowest Minimum Wage Province
excerpt: Alberta once led the country with the highest minimum wage in Canada. But after reaching $15/hour in 2018, the province hit pause and never pressed play again. Now, as other provinces push past that mark, Alberta is poised to have the lowest minimum wage in the country.
author: Culture Alberta
